title: Epic: Dashboard Client Installation and Update UX Improvements
body:
Implements the following enhancements to the Liferay Tunnel Dashboard Overview and installation guide modal:
1. Configurable package manager visibility (Brew/Scoop) via server-config.
2. Dynamic CLI installation block displaying local upgrade commands (`lfr-tunnel -upgrade`) when an active client is detected.
3. Styling/spacing fixes for the Update Available header and CLI details.

issues.title: Sub-Issue #1: Implement configurable package manager options (Homebrew, Scoop) on Server and UI
issues.body:
Add configuration parameters to control the visibility of Scoop and Homebrew in the installation guide:
- Add `disable_brew` and `disable_scoop` config options in `server-config.yaml` / Go struct.
- Expose these configuration states in the `/api/version` JSON response.
- Conditionally hide the corresponding Homebrew and Scoop sections/tabs in the frontend Dashboard UI installer guide modal based on these config settings.

issues.title: Sub-Issue #2: Implement dynamic upgrade command recommendation in Dashboard
issues.body:
When a client has already connected and we detect a version mismatch:
- Check if `currentUser.last_client_version` is present.
- If present, show a simplified dynamic upgrade card with `lfr-tunnel -upgrade` instead of the full installation command script.
- Retain the SHA256 checksum and the 'Other OSs' modal trigger.

issues.title: Sub-Issue #3: Correct layout spacing and styling in the CLI Client Installation dashboard block
issues.body:
Improve the visual presentation of the dashboard overview client installation card:
- Increase margins between the header ('Update Available') and the version subtext.
- Fine-tune padding and line-heights for optimal readability.
